창세기 43:1.content: The famine was severe in the land.

창세기 43:2.content: When they had eaten up the grain which they had brought out of Egypt, their father said to them, “Go again, buy us a little more food.”

창세기 43:3.content: Judah spoke to him, saying, “The man solemnly warned us, saying, ‘You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.’

창세기 43:4.content: If you’ll send our brother with us, we’ll go down and buy you food;

창세기 43:5.content: but if you don’t send him, we won’t go down, for the man said to us, ‘You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.’ ”

창세기 43:6.content: Israel said, “Why did you treat me so badly, telling the man that you had another brother?”

창세기 43:7.content: They said, “The man asked directly concerning ourselves, and concerning our relatives, saying, ‘Is your father still alive? Have you another brother?’ We just answered his questions. Is there any way we could know that he would say, ‘Bring your brother down?’ ”

창세기 43:8.content: Judah said to Israel, his father, “Send the boy with me, and we’ll get up and go, so that we may live, and not die, both we, and you, and also our little ones.

창세기 43:9.content: I’ll be collateral for him. From my hand will you require him. If I don’t bring him to you, and set him before you, then let me bear the blame forever;

창세기 43:10.content: for if we hadn’t delayed, surely we would have returned a second time by now.”

창세기 43:11.content: Their father, Israel, said to them, “If it must be so, then do this: Take from the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry down a present for the man, a little balm, a little honey, spices and myrrh, nuts, and almonds;

창세기 43:12.content: and take double money in your hand, and take back the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks. Perhaps it was an oversight.

창세기 43:13.content: Take your brother also, get up, and return to the man.

창세기 43:14.content: May God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may release to you your other brother and Benjamin. If I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.”

창세기 43:15.content: The men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and Benjamin; and got up, went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.

창세기 43:16.content: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, “Bring the men into the house, and butcher an animal, and prepare; for the men will dine with me at noon.”

창세기 43:17.content: The man did as Joseph commanded, and the man brought the men to Joseph’s house.

창세기 43:18.content: The men were afraid, because they were brought to Joseph’s house; and they said, “Because of the money that was returned in our sacks the first time, we’re brought in; that he may seek occasion against us, attack us, and seize us as slaves, along with our donkeys.”

창세기 43:19.content: They came near to the steward of Joseph’s house, and they spoke to him at the door of the house,

창세기 43:20.content: and said, “Oh, my lord, we indeed came down the first time to buy food.

창세기 43:21.content: When we came to the lodging place, we opened our sacks, and behold, each man’s money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight. We have brought it back in our hand.

창세기 43:22.content: We have brought down other money in our hand to buy food. We don’t know who put our money in our sacks.”

창세기 43:23.content: He said, “Peace be to you. Don’t be afraid. Your God, and the God of your father, has given you treasure in your sacks. I received your money.” He brought Simeon out to them.

창세기 43:24.content: The man brought the men into Joseph’s house, and gave them water, and they washed their feet. He gave their donkeys fodder.

창세기 43:25.content: They prepared the present for Joseph’s coming at noon, for they heard that they should eat bread there.

창세기 43:26.content: When Joseph came home, they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house, and bowed themselves down to the earth before him.

창세기 43:27.content: He asked them of their welfare, and said, “Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he yet alive?”

창세기 43:28.content: They said, “Your servant, our father, is well. He is still alive.” They bowed down humbly.

창세기 43:29.content: He lifted up his eyes, and saw Benjamin, his brother, his mother’s son, and said, “Is this your youngest brother, of whom you spoke to me?” He said, “God be gracious to you, my son.”

창세기 43:30.content: Joseph hurried, for his heart yearned over his brother; and he sought a place to weep. He entered into his room, and wept there.

창세기 43:31.content: He washed his face, and came out. He controlled himself, and said, “Serve the meal.”

창세기 43:32.content: They served him by himself, and them by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves, because the Egyptians don’t eat with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination to the Egyptians.

창세기 43:33.content: They sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his youth, and the men marveled with one another.

창세기 43:34.content: He sent portions to them from before him, but Benjamin’s portion was five times as much as any of theirs. They drank, and were merry with him.
